opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My partner said I passed out in my sleep and was making horrible gasping noises. I feel fine now, just exhausted.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My partner was terrified and called for help — I don't really remember any of it, I just woke up confused. I feel okay now but they're worried something is really wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: in the middle of the night, while I was asleep
symptom_narrative.duration: my partner says the whole thing lasted about a minute, then I was confused for maybe ten minutes
symptom_narrative.character: I have no memory of it. My partner says I suddenly went limp and unresponsive and was making loud gasping, snoring-type breathing noises, then I came round on my own
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: I feel completely wiped out and a bit shaken, but physically okay now
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: nothing specific — I just blacked out
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it was a single episode overnight; nothing like it before
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I recovered on my own after a few minutes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I don't know — I was asleep when it happened
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain and no racing heart that I noticed. I've had a bit of a cold and felt feverish today, and I took some paracetamol for it.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never fainted before in my life
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I was completely well and active up until this — work, exercise, everything normal

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: a couple of tablets earlier today for the fever

family_history_as_reported.narrative: My brother died suddenly in his sleep when he was 30. No one ever really found out why — it was a huge shock, he seemed healthy.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with partner who witnessed the episode and made the call; usually fit and active

objective_data_offered.value: felt hot and shivery, home thermometer read around 38 earlier
objective_data_offered.timing: earlier this evening before the episode
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer; taken before paracetamol

objective_data_offered.value: looks pale and drained but is awake, talking normally and knows where he is
partner says he was confused and groggy for about ten minutes afterwards but is back to normal now, with no jerking movements, no bitten tongue and no wetting seen during the episode
